candidates.evidence_quote: In fiscal 2024, we recorded impairment of assets and lease termination expenses of $13.6 million primarily related to impairment of long-lived assets for one The Cheesecake Factory (previously partially impaired) and six Other FRC locations

lease termination income, net for four The Cheesecake Factory restaurants, one Grand Lux Cafe location, one Flower Child location, one Social Monk location and one Other FRC location (that closed in early fiscal 2025)
one Other FRC location (that closed in early fiscal 2025)
Total revenues were $921.0 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024 compared to $877.0 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2023.
Net income and diluted net income per share were $41.2 million and $0.83, respectively, in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024.
Comparable restaurant sales at The Cheesecake Factory restaurants increased 1.7% year-over-year in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024.
North Italia operating information: Comparable restaurant sales vs. prior year 1 % 7 % 2 % 8 %
ongoing outperformance in comparable sales and traffic at The Cheesecake Factory restaurants versus the broader casual dining industry
We continued to capture market share, as demonstrated by the ongoing outperformance in comparable sales and traffic at The Cheesecake Factory restaurants versus the broader casual dining industry.
Consumer demand for the distinct, high-quality dining experiences we provide our guests across our experiential concepts reinforces our confidence in the long-term growth potential of our portfolio.

During the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024, the Company opened nine new restaurants, including three North Italia locations, two Flower Child locations, two FRC restaurants and the relocation of two Cheesecake Factory restaurants.

The Company now expects to open as many as 25 new restaurants in fiscal 2025, including as many as three to four The Cheesecake Factory restaurants, six to seven North Italia locations, six to seven Flower Child locations, and as many as eight to nine FRC restaurants.
the achievement of our key financial and operational objectives, including comparable sales growth, margin expansion and accelerating accretive unit growth

Represents changes in the fair value of the deferred consideration and contingent consideration and compensation liabilities related to the North Italia and FRC acquisition, as well as amortization of acquired definite-lived licensing agreements.
The Company repurchased approximately 11,800 shares of its stock at a cost of $0.5 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024.
the Company’s Board of Directors has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.27 per share to be paid on March 18, 2025
$345 million in principal amount of 0.375% convertible senior notes due June 2026 and $110 million in principal amount drawn on the Company’s revolving credit facility

notes: No fiscal_kpis on this chunk (only chunk_001 carries them); all candidates are event-text-grounded.

Reviewed both events in order: 10-K 0001410578-25-000195 part 6/6 (critical accounting policies: impairment, lease terminations, leases) and 8-K 0001104659-25-015185 (Q4 FY2024 press release with full financial tables and safe-harbor list).
revenue_surprise and eps_surprise use the EARNINGS {metric}_surprise convention for reported quarterly results; the press release itself does not quote a consensus figure.

same_store_sales is used for The Cheesecake Factory flagship comps (brand name equals company legal name, which is banned inside driver_name); north_italia_same_store_sales kept as a separate brand-level driver per ontology R9.

Candidates 22-30 come from the safe-harbor risk-factor list (boilerplate but names specific causal variables: minimum wage, tariffs, FX, supply chain, consumer confidence, unemployment, interest rates, inflation, dining restrictions); reviewers may prune.
inflation is a single-word name; kept because it is a specific standard macro variable, not a bare category word, but flagged for G2 review.

Skipped (10): incremental_borrowing_rate and treasury_yield_discount_rate (accounting-mechanical lease/impairment assumptions, not market drivers), adverse weather (polarity word + vague), armed conflicts (generic list item), landlord economic health (vague), debt covenant compliance (vague), tax reform legislation (boilerplate), G&A expense line (minor line item, no narrative), third-party bakery business (descriptive About-section text, no causal claim), international licensing footprint (descriptive only).
timing of new unit development and related permitting deduped into new_restaurant_development / unit_development_guidance.
